title: ornus cuspidata
intro: Ornus cuspidata, also known as the hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Northern Hemisphere, characterized by its thorny branches and small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Ornus cuspidata lie in its berries, leaves, and flowers, which have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and heart failure, due to their high content of flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins. The health benefits of Ornus cuspidata include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Ornus cuspidata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat digestive issues and anxiety.
